_meta.purpose: On-device measured speed and VRAM on the reference RTX 5090. This is the layer no public benchmark publishes. Numbers here are MEASURED, not inferred.

machine.name: NOMAD (pc-nomade, RTX 5090)
machine.gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090, 32 GB GDDR7, compute capability 12.0 (Blackwell)

environment.loader: spandrel ModelLoader (the same path ComfyUI uses)

method.input: synthetic 1280x720 random tensor (speed and VRAM are content-independent for conv upscalers; quality is NOT assessed here)
method.timing: median of 5 forward passes after 1 warmup, torch.cuda.synchronize around each
method.note_resolution: x4 of 720p = 5120x2880, which overshoots 4K (3840x2160). A true 720p->4K (downscale after) is slightly cheaper; 720p->1080p via tiling/resize is much faster. These are the eager full-frame x4 numbers.

measurements.note: VAE decode ~73s of 85s dominates; DiT upscale ~2s; near the 32 GB ceiling
OOM > 32 GB at batch 9 even with decode tiling (VAE encode inflation blew up)

measurements.config: vae encode+decode tiled, blocks_to_swap 16, dit_offload_device cpu
measurements.note: 4K FITS in ~9 GB and is efficient with batch 1 + tiling + offload
7B at batch 5 + offload is FASTER than 3B at batch 9 (batching dominates, not model size)
WORKS; near the 32GB ceiling; wall includes model load; FlashVSR needs >=21 frames

BLOCKED on 32GB: OOM without tiling; the tiled-dit and full paths hit a FlashVSR_plus repo bug (UnboundLocalError temp_name); tiny-long+tiled-vae maxes 32GB (142s) but produced no output. 4x impractical on 32GB with this build.
Topaz classic via the local tvai ffmpeg CLI (-f null), steady-state on a 100-frame clip (load amortized). VRAM-light.
Confirms the community ~4.7-7 fps @4x. Far faster and lighter than the diffusion tier.

Iris/Gaia 25-frame (load-included ~2.7-3.0 fps); same featherweight VRAM class. Starlight DIFFUSION is NOT measurable via the tvai ffmpeg CLI (needs Neuroserver). Auth runs offline from cached auth.tpz despite cancelled subscriptions.

insights: SeedVR2 throughput on the 5090 is dominated by VAE-decode batching, not model size or target resolution: decoding a 9-frame batch in one shot (~73s) is the bottleneck, while small batches + VAE tiling decode far faster.

Counterintuitive measured result: 3B fp8 1080p batch 9 = 0.11 fps / 31.4 GB, but 3B fp8 4K batch 1 (tiled + offload) = 0.13 fps / 9 GB, and 7B fp8 1080p batch 5 (offload) = 0.35 fps / 25.5 GB. The efficient path is small batch + VAE tiling + block-swap with --dit_offload_device cpu.
32 GB is the binding constraint at large temporal batches: 3B fp8 batch 9 nearly maxes the card at 1080p and OOMs at 4K. Use batch 1-5 + tiling + offload for 4K.
fp16 doubles speed and halves VRAM vs fp32 for the ESRGAN/GAN tier (free win).

All SeedVR2 runs: SageAttention 3 + Flash Attention + Triton active (Blackwell-optimized), torch 2.7+cu128, on the 9-frame synthetic 720p clip. Times are end-to-end inference (load + encode + DiT + decode + post).

FlashVSR (FlashVSR_plus build, isolated venv torch 2.9+cu128, SageAttention) on the 5090: tiny x2 (720p->1440p) works at ~0.80 fps near the 32GB ceiling, but x4 (720p->2880p, 4K-class) is blocked, OOM without tiling and the tiled-dit / full paths hit repo bugs. The marketed '~17 fps on A100' does not translate to anything near real-time at these settings. FlashVSR requires >= 21 input frames (streaming window).

Topaz classic (Proteus/Iris/Gaia) on the 5090 via the local tvai ffmpeg CLI: steady-state Proteus = 15 fps at 1080p and 7.3 fps at 4K, peak VRAM only ~1.8-2.7 GB. That is roughly 20-100x faster and 4-15x lighter than the diffusion tier, and confirms the community ~4.7-7 fps@4x. The classic-vs-diffusion gap is the central practical trade-off: classic is near-interactive and featherweight but only sharpens; diffusion rebuilds detail but is overnight-slow and VRAM-bound. Topaz Starlight (diffusion) was not measurable via this CLI (needs Neuroserver).
pending: SeedVR2 + FlashVSR DONE (FlashVSR x4 blocked by OOM/repo bugs on 32GB; tiny x2 works). Remaining diffusion: SUPIR, CCSR, Clarity; plus SeedVR2 7B at 4K and fp16 variants.
Quality assessment on real AI-generated 720p frames (LTX/Wan/Veo/Seedance/Kling), separate from these throughput/VRAM numbers.
SeedVR2 measured via the node's inference_cli.py on a 9-frame synthetic 720p clip (sageattn_3); per-run logs kept on NOMAD (sv_*.log).
